title: A peaceful rebellion: choosing the bed
subtitle: A short visualisation for making the act of going to bed feel like a choice, not a defeat.
excerpt: A brief visualisation from Day 13 of the SLEEP program. The frame is deliberate: going to bed is a small act of defiance against the productivity voice, done with satisfaction rather than guilt.
sourceNotes: Original to homemade.education as a companion visualisation for Day 13 of SLEEP: A 30-Day Tapping Intensive to Fall Asleep Faster and Wake Energised (Rebecca J Page, 2025).

content.text: A brief visualisation from Day 13 of the SLEEP program. The practice reframes going to bed: not as giving in, not as escaping the list, but as a deliberate choice you make on purpose.
Sit for a moment before you get into bed. Close your eyes or soften your gaze. Three slow breaths.

Picture the to-do list, the unfinished things, the things that could have been done, the things tomorrow will need. Now picture yourself, deliberately, turning away from the list. Not frantically, not guiltily. Just turning.

You pick up the bed. You choose it. Not because you've finished (there is always more), but because this is what a person who respects their body does. The list will be there tomorrow. Your body needs this now.
Picture yourself getting into bed with the same quiet satisfaction you'd feel after finishing a task well. This is the task. You're doing it.
Open your eyes and get into bed.
Original to homemade.education. Pairs with Day 13 of SLEEP, Rebecca J Page's 2025 30-day tapping intensive for sleep.
